verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase with an N-terminal myristoylation site and a palmitoylation motif that direct it to the plasma membrane. It is stably anchored to the inner leaflet of the PM via lipidation and is a key signaling node in B-cell receptor (BCR) and Fc-receptor signaling. While canonically described as a cytoplasmic kinase, LYN's myristoylation and palmitoylation are constitutive and its substrate-binding surface and catalytic domain are accessible from the cytoplasm facing the inner leaflet, making it an integral PM-resident signaling protein. It is also a well-validated therapeutic target on B-lymphoid cells via surface-directed inhibitors and immune-engaging modalities, confirming PM localization.
